vocab.example: I much prefer being at the helm because it gives me a sense of autonomy over the pace and direction of the journey.

vocab.meaning: người ngồi sau hay chỉ đạo lái xe (nghĩa bóng: người hay can thiệp)
vocab.example: I try my best not to be a backseat driver when I'm a passenger, as I know how irritating unsolicited advice can be.

vocab.meaning: giao phó trách nhiệm (cho người khác)
vocab.example: On long road trips, I’m happy to relegate the responsibility of driving to someone else so I can simply enjoy the scenery.

vocab.meaning: một trải nghiệm căng thẳng tột độ
vocab.example: For some, navigating through heavy urban traffic is a nerve-racking experience, which is why they opt to be passengers.
Being a passenger allows me to unwind and decompress after a long day at work without worrying about road rage.

vocab.meaning: có được một tầm nhìn bao quát
vocab.example: As a driver, you have to stay focused on the tarmac, whereas a passenger can truly command a view of the surrounding landscape.
I enjoy being the driver because it allows me to put my navigational skills to the test in unfamiliar territories.
I prefer to drive because being behind the wheel actually helps mitigate my tendency to suffer from motion sickness.

vocab.meaning: tính chất ít vận động/ngồi một chỗ
vocab.example: The sedentary nature of being a passenger can be a bit tedious for me, as I prefer the active engagement of driving.

vocab.meaning: có quyền quyết định cuối cùng
vocab.example: As the driver, you have the final say on the music selection and the temperature of the air conditioning.
